commit.message:
feat: initial Az.AI.Workbench PowerShell module

Application-layer multi-agent orchestration for Azure AI Foundry.

Cmdlets:
- Connect-AzAIFoundry: connect to Foundry direct or AI Workbench instance
- New-AzAIConversation: create conversation for shared-conversation patterns
- Invoke-AzAIAgent: invoke agent (agent reference or direct mode)
- Invoke-AzAIFanOut: parallel fan-out to isolated specialist conversations
- Invoke-AzAISynthesize: synthesize branch results on shared conversation
- Get-AzAIAgent: list available agents
- Import-AzAIManifest: load YAML agent manifests locally

Two connection modes (Foundry Direct, AI Workbench)
Two execution modes (Agent Reference, Direct Mode)
Three examples (basic chat, agent switching, fan-out + synthesize)

when image digests haven't changed.

Defer MaybeValid fully_validated clear until after staleness check

ballot.process_envelope() returns, gated on result != Invalid. Prevents
stale replayed MaybeValid envelopes from suppressing local SCP state
emission. Mirrors stellar-core where isNewerStatement() rejects stale
envelopes before the MaybeValid branch.

Fixes #1161

commit.message:
Add built-in AssignTask command and AgentTaskAssigned event for AgenticAggregate (#329)

* Add detailed plan for AssignTask support in AgenticAggregate

Created plans/assign-task/assign-task-plan.md with 5-phase design:
- Phase 1: RequestingParty sealed interface (api module)
- Phase 2: AssignTaskCommand and AgentTaskAssignedEvent (agentic module)
- Phase 3: Built-in handlers and registration (agentic module)

commit.message:
fix: prevent index.db bloat with auto_vacuum and startup maintenance (#378)

Enable SQLite incremental auto_vacuum so freed pages are reused for new
writes instead of accumulating on the freelist permanently. Add a
maintenance() method that runs FTS optimize, incremental_vacuum, WAL
checkpoint, and ANALYZE — time-gated to fire only on the first indexing
pass after startup (4-hour throttle), making it a complete no-op during
the 5-second auto-refresh cycles.

Root causes addressed:
- auto_vacuum=NONE caused 200 MB of permanent freelist waste (35% of DB)
- FTS5 segments never optimized during incremental indexing
- WAL never explicitly checkpointed after heavy writes

Changes:
- index_db/mod.rs: ensure_incremental_auto_vacuum() with one-time VACUUM
  migration, maintenance()/maintenance_force()/run_full_maintenance()
  with epoch-based throttling, Migration 10 for maintenance_state table
- lib.rs: reindex_search_content calls time-gated maintenance(),
  rebuild_search_content calls maintenance_force() (bypass throttle)
- 147 tests pass, zero warnings

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: change API ingress to INGRESS_TRAFFIC_ALL and add workflow_dispatch to deploy

Cloud Run-to-Cloud Run calls in the same project are NOT automatically
treated as internal traffic unless VPC egress is configured. Without a
Serverless VPC Access Connector or Direct VPC Egress on the UI service,
requests from the nginx BFF are seen as external by the API ingress and
rejected with 404.

Security is maintained by Cloud Run IAM: the API requires a valid OIDC
token from osrs-ui-sa (roles/run.invoker). Unauthenticated public
requests receive 403.

Also adds workflow_dispatch to the deploy workflow so the ingress change
can be applied without triggering a full image rebuild.

commit.message:
fix(minimax): fix doubled audio in streaming mode

DashScope SSE for MiniMax TTS sends accumulated output where the final
event contains the complete audio. Blindly concatenating all events
caused the audio to repeat. Added detection: if the last SSE chunk is
>= half the total buffer size, it contains the complete audio and we
use only that chunk instead of the concatenation.
